verStmt.version: v.1: Edited, anonymised data for public distribution

stdyInfo.abstract: The Post-Apartheid Socio-Economic Series (PASES) is a stacked series of 28 nationally-representative cross-sectional household surveys covering every year between 1995-2024, with the exception of 2000 and 2001. The source data consists of data from the October Household Surveys conducted every year by Statistics South Africa between 1995-19991 and the General Household Surveys, conducted every year by StatsSA since 2002. The current PASES includes data up to 2024. The surveys collect both household and person information and the series currently consists of just over 640 000 households and their 2.4 million members. A subset of variables from the survey data has been included and harmonised across time. Along with some new and derived variables, there are currently 119 variables in the dataset covering social, demographic and economic outcomes. The data are released in two files, a main file and an extra income variables file along with the syntax files used to create the data.

sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation of the data is Province
sumDscr.universe: The survey covers all de jure household members (usual residents) of households in the nine provinces of South Africa, and residents in workers' hostels. The survey does not cover collective living quarters such as student hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons, and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes: PASES covers a variety of socio-economic and demographic outcomes in one dataset at both the person and household level. The focus of this first version of PASES is on labour market variables, mainly labour market status, and the harmonising, weighting and imputing of earnings information. The data also includes a consolidated set of variables on demographic and household characteristics, geography, education, household income, social grants, fertility, and transport.

dataColl.actMin: PASES is based on anonymised publicly available from Statistics South Africa.

useStmt.citReq: Kerr, A., Thornton, A., and Wittenberg, M. Post-Apartheid Socio-Economic Series 1995-2024 [dataset]. Version 1. Cape Town: DataFirst [producer and distributor], 2025. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/49GK-P872
useStmt.conditions: Public access data for use under a Creative Commons CC-BY (Attribution-only) License

var.name: quarter
var.labl: quarter of the year in which interview occurred

var.name: interviewmonth
var.labl: approximate month of interview derived from statssa metadata

var.name: proxyrespno
var.labl: question respondent: person number (2009 onwards with some exceptions)

var.name: marstat
var.labl: marital status with cohabiting; note 2002-4 omit cohabiting

catgry.labl: 17. certificate with less than grade 12 / std 10
18. diploma with less than grade 12 / std 10
26. post higher diploma (m-tech and master's degree)

var.name: hhinc
var.labl: household income monthly rand amount: total

var.name: realhhinc
var.labl: real household income monthly rand amount: total (dec 2023 rands)

var.name: hh_grant
var.labl: household income source: grants, excl social relief grant

catgry.labl: 6. sales of farming products and services

var.name: grant_income
var.labl: social grants: total individual social grant rand income

var.name: realhhgrantinc0
var.labl: hh income component: household grant income with zeroes imputed, monthly real

var.name: realhhreminc0
var.labl: hh income component: household remittance income with zeroes imputed, monthly re

var.name: realhhpensioninc0
var.labl: hh income component: household pension income with zeroes imputed, monthly real

var.name: realhhearnings0
var.labl: hh income component: household earnings income with zeroes imputed, monthly real

var.name: oap_g
var.labl: social grants: old age pension receipt

var.name: cs_g
var.labl: social grants: child support grant receipt

var.name: foster_g
var.labl: social grants: foster care grants receipt

var.name: gia
var.labl: social grants: grant in aid receipt

var.name: soc_rel
var.labl: social grants: social relief grant receipt

var.name: warvet_g
var.labl: social grants: war vet. grant receipt

var.name: srd_g
var.labl: social grants: covid-19 social relief of distress grant receipt

var.name: cstopup_g
var.labl: social grants: csg top up for orphans receipt

var.name: oap_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: old age pension rand income

var.name: dis_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: disability grant rand income

var.name: foster_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: foster care grants rand income

var.name: warvet_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: war vet. grant rand income

var.name: cs_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: child support grant rand income

var.name: gia_inc
var.labl: social grants: grant in aid rand income

var.name: caredep_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: care dependency rand income

var.name: cstopup_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: csg top up for orphans rand income

var.name: srd_g_inc
var.labl: social grants: covid-19 srd rand income

var.name: emp_return
var.labl: employment: have work to return to

catgry.labl: 1. in the formal sector (registered to perform activity)
2. in the informal sector (not registered to perform activit

var.name: earningsmp
var.labl: earnings: nominal monthly earnings with bracket midpoints

var.name: realearningsmp
var.labl: earnings: real earnings with bracket midpoints in december 2023 rands

var.name: outlier
var.labl: earnings: real earnings outlier flag; zeroes flagged as outliers

catgry.labl: 4. ended before the child was born (miscarriage/ child died
5. ended before the child was born (termination of pregnancy

var.name: own_car
var.labl: transport: the household owns a car

var.name: commutemode
var.labl: transport: usual means of transport to get to place of work

catgry.labl: 7. lift club by a group of people sharing a private vehicle
8. own car / other private vehicle/company vehicle

var.name: commutetime
var.labl: transport: time it takes to get to place of work in minutes

var.name: hhbustrips
var.labl: transport: total number bus trips taken by households in last week

var.name: hhbuscost
var.labl: transport: how much spent on bus trips by household in last week

var.name: hhbusdist
var.labl: transport: distance to nearest bus stop in km

var.name: hhtraintrips
var.labl: transport: total number train trips taken by households in last week

var.name: hhtraincost
var.labl: transport: how much spent on train trips by household in last week

var.name: hhtraindist
var.labl: transport: distance to nearest train station in km

var.name: hhtaxitrips
var.labl: transport: total number taxi trips taken by households in last week

var.name: hhtaxicost
var.labl: transport: how much spent on taxi trips by household in last week

var.name: hhtaxidist
var.labl: transport: distance to nearest taxi stop in km

var.name: hhinc_imputed
var.labl: household income monthly rand amount: total, with imputations

var.name: realhhinc_imputed
var.labl: real household income monthly rand amount: total (dec 2023 rands), with imputati

var.name: realhhimputedearnings0
var.labl: hh income component: household earnings income with zeroes + missings imputed, m

var.name: bracketweight
var.labl: datafirst weight to correct for earnings bracket responses

var.name: realearnings
var.labl: earnings: real earnings in december 2023 rands

var.name: imputed_realearningsmp
var.labl: earnings: real earnings with bracket midpoints (dec 2023), missings imputed

var.name: imputed_earningsmp
var.labl: earnings: nominal earnings with bracket midpoints (dec 2023), missings imputed
